name: Housing Initiative Partnership, Inc.(Hip)
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor and affordable housing developer in Maryland with 35+ years of community impact. Offers free homebuyer education, financial counseling, and down payment assistance.
description_long:
Housing Initiative Partnership, Inc. (HIP) is a Maryland-based non-profit organization founded over 35 years ago that combines affordable housing development with comprehensive financial and housing counseling services. Operating offices in Hyattsville (Prince George's County), Germantown, and Gaithersburg (Montgomery County), HIP has established itself as a community-focused organization addressing housing insecurity and financial instability.

HIP offers a broad range of free and low-cost services including HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops, down payment and closing cost assistance for first-time buyers, loan modification counseling, energy assistance programs, and rental assistance through community schools. The organization also provides financial counseling, home sharing program coordination, and connections to energy efficiency rebates. Their services are available in multiple languages including English, Amharic, French, and Spanish to serve diverse populations.

What distinguishes HIP is their dual model: they function both as a housing counselor (providing free financial guidance) and as a developer of actual affordable housing projects. They actively construct and sell net-zero energy homes, operate senior housing communities, and develop mixed-income neighborhoods. This allows them to provide hands-on counseling backed by direct access to affordable homeownership opportunities. Testimonials highlight personalized attention, advocacy with utility programs, and connections to tax credits and energy efficiency programs that generate measurable savings for clients.

HIP is best suited for Maryland residents (particularly in Prince George's and Montgomery counties) seeking non-predatory guidance on homeownership, rental assistance, or financial stability. However, their services are geographically limited to Maryland and focused on their service areas. While they offer genuine free counseling backed by non-profit expertise, access requires being in their operational zones.
pros: Free homebuyer education workshops offered multiple times monthly with virtual and in-person options
Down payment and closing cost assistance specifically for first-time homebuyers—reduces barriers to ownership
HUD-approved housing counseling with certified advisors providing personalized loan modification help
Connections to concrete assistance programs: homeowner tax credits, energy assistance, energy efficiency upgrades at no cost
Multilingual services (English, Amharic, French, Spanish) serving diverse immigrant and refugee communities
Actual affordable housing development creates inventory of homes clients can purchase after counseling
Rental assistance program through community schools for families in Prince George's and Montgomery counties
cons: Service area limited to specific Maryland regions only—not available outside Prince George's and Montgomery counties plus service areas
Majority of program information requires visiting office or calling; limited online self-service or digital application options
Community schools rental assistance eligibility appears to require student enrollment, excluding other families
No indication of evening/weekend counseling hours for working individuals outside business hours
Website does not clearly detail counselor credentials, certification levels, or response time expectations
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Prince George's and Montgomery counties seeking down payment assistance and financial counseling
Homeowners struggling with mortgage payments, loan modifications, or utility bills in Maryland service areas
Immigrant and refugee families needing multilingual financial guidance and rental assistance
Community school families qualifying for rental assistance in HIP's partner school program
diagnosis: HIP is best for Maryland residents in Prince George's and Montgomery counties seeking free, professional, non-profit housing and financial counseling combined with actual affordable homeownership opportunities. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—services are not available outside their Maryland service areas, and applicants must be able to access in-person or virtual appointments during business hours.
services: HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops (virtual and in-person)
Down payment and closing cost assistance for first-time homebuyers
Loan modification counseling and mortgage default prevention
Free financial counseling and money management education
Energy assistance programs (utility bill payment support)
Energy efficiency rebate and appliance replacement programs
Rental assistance through community schools partnership
Homeowner tax credit identification and application
Community solar program enrollment for utility bill savings
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 620 East Diamond Avenue, Suite L, Gaithersburg, MD 20877
